zh.lifeSummary: 當George Vessey於1535年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其時，他的父親Charles Yeardley Vessey28歲，母親Elizabeth Doyle30歲。他於1558年與Marion Woodward在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他於1579年住在英國爾瓦維頓。他去世於1593年在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: Vessey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vessey (Manche), named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Vessius or Vettius (of uncertain origin) + the locative suffix -acum. There has been some confusion with Veazey .

variants.name: Veazey
meanings: English (Rutland): nickname from Anglo-Norman French enveisé ‘playful’ (Old French envoisié), Latinized as invesiatus (compare medieval Latin invasus ‘possessed by a demon’) and paralleled by lascivus ‘wanton’. The first syllable was lost early, resulting in numerous dialectal and orthographic variants; only Lenfestey preserves the fuller form, with an intrusive medial -t-. There has also been some confusion with Vessey .
History: John Veazey came from England to MD in the late 17th century. Thomas Ward Veazey (born 1774) was a MD legislator and planter.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When George Vessey was born in 1535, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Charles Yeardley Vessey, was 28 and his mother, Elizabeth Doyle, was 30. He married Marion Woodward in 1558, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He lived in Waverton, Cheshire, England in 1579. He died in 1593, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

Il a épousé Marion Woodward en 1558, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il a vécu à Waverton, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1579. ko.Oxford, Suffolk, England: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 스퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: George Vessey(이)가 1535년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Charles Yeardley Vessey(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Doyle(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1558년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 Marion Woodward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1579년도에 영국 체실르 와버턴에서 살았다. 그는 1593년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Oxford, Suffolk, England: оксфорд, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Vessey родился в 1535 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Charles Yeardley Vessey было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Doyle, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Marion Woodward в 1558 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он жил в местности вавиртон, чешир, Англия в 1579 году. Он умер в 1593 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет.
